message: Built frontend-only Vue page editor (Tasks 1-11, subagent-driven). Stack settled: Vite+Vue3+TS, Pinia, Tailwind v4 (CSS-first), shadcn-vue on reka-ui, @lucide/vue (lucide-vue-next deprecated), hand-rolled drag/resize (no DnD lib). usePagesStore is the draft page-state contract. Publish/upload/drawing/device-API deliberately stubbed. 42 tests pass.

I1+M5 done: position-aware timeline reorder (drop tile onto another -> takes its slot index; sidebar page drops still append) + interaction tests (mounted drag updates store, reorder both directions, ToolRail localStorage wiring). 46 tests pass.

Prototype feedback round: draw-to-place element creation (tool click only activates; drag a rect on canvas to create), working pen (DrawingLayer capture -> DrawingEl/DrawingWidget SVG), size-scaled clock/calendar fonts (clock capped by width), Backspace/Delete + ToolRail trash deletes selected element. 55 tests pass; verified in real browser at scale 1.2.

Feedback round 3 (6 subagent-driven changes + review fixes, commits 21009bd..c66036b): unified global colour panel (colour on BaseEl, setElementColour); pen stroke scales on resize (natW/natH viewBox); live element creation with auto-switch-to-select and pointer-events gating so creation tools always create; drawings in thumbnails; smooth pen via perfect-freehand (filled ink paths, tap-to-dot). Backspace+Delete both verified deleting in-browser. 81 tests pass.
Target display pinned: Waveshare 7.3in E6 (Spectra 6), 800x480, 6-colour, full-refresh only. Persisted from round-one design doc into context/hardware.md + decisions.md.

Device render approach = C: ONE renderer written in Rust, compiled to WASM for the editor (draws to canvas) and native for the device (draws to framebuffer). Single renderer => pixel parity by construction, no two-codebase parity treadmill. Chosen partly as a deliberate learn-Rust / over-engineer-for-fun call. Replaces the editor's Vue HTML/CSS widgets with WASM-drawn pixels; Vue keeps the editing chrome.

SUPERSEDES the earlier 'approach C' decision. Render approach = A: TWO renderers. Editor keeps its Vue render as a rough DESIGN surface (allowed to be approximate). Device renderer is SEPARATE, written in Rust (the fun/learn-Rust goal lives here). No pixel-perfect editor<->device parity requirement — only 'no glaring differences'. The device serves its real 800x480 6-colour render over a web endpoint, which is the authoritative 'what it looks like' preview (and the hardware stand-in pre-delivery), so the editor needs no image-quantise preview of its own. Pixel-comparison tests become a generous-threshold guardrail against glaring divergence, not a tautology.

Refresh-driven product simplification (after watching real Spectra 6 refresh speed): (1) DROP the clock element entirely — nothing updates minute-to-minute. (2) Keep the DATE, as a calendar variant. (3) DROP the timeline/auto-loop — the panel is too slow to cycle pages. (4) Pages model = (b): keep MULTIPLE saved layouts (drafts), publish ONE as the live display; no auto-cycle. Consequence: the device render trigger collapses to 'render on publish + one scheduled re-render at midnight for the date rollover' — the clock-vs-refresh tension is gone. Ripples into the existing editor: remove Timeline/TimelineItem, ClockWidget/ClockOptions, clock tool in ToolRail, clock in thumbnails, timeline reorder; add a date-only calendar variant; DocState drops 'timeline', gains a 'live page' pointer.

Device storage model = (1): the device stores the WHOLE document (all draft pages + livePageId + uploaded images) and is the single source of truth. Editor GETs the full document to hydrate, POSTs the full document to publish, marks one page live. Drafts are shared across any browser that opens the editor. Rejected (2) live-page-only (drafts would be trapped per-browser, wrong for a shared board).

Editor hosting = (a): the device serves the built Vue editor + the API + the preview, all one origin on the LAN (http://corkboard.local/), no internet needed. Dev workflow: Vite dev server proxies API/preview to a running device-server instance. FUTURE (deferred, keep simple now): owner has ideas for decoupling the editor from the display/device — revisit later; do not build for it yet.

Routine stack calls (owner delegated): device web framework = axum; on-device storage = plain files (document.json + images/ dir, GC unreferenced images on publish); renderer crates to be specified in the design doc.

Calendar events source = B: external feed via Google Calendar's secret iCal (ICS) URL. The device fetches the ICS over HTTPS and parses it; events are resolved AT RENDER TIME on the device, NOT stored in the document. CalendarEl stops carrying a frozen events[] list and instead references a feed + filter (today/week/date variant). Refresh cadence: re-fetch + re-render at the midnight tick and on publish (no new refresh machinery; once-a-day freshness is fine for slow e-paper). Editor preview shows SAMPLE/placeholder events (rough design surface); device preview shows the real ones. NEW CATEGORY introduced: device config/secrets. The secret iCal URL is a credential -> lives in device-side config, kept OUT of the published document and OUT of git. Rejected C (full Google OAuth) as too heavy for a household board.

REFINES the render-trigger decision. Refresh model = decouple feed-polling from panel-refresh. (1) Poll the iCal feed on an interval, default 60min, settable in device config. (2) On every poll/publish/manual-trigger: render the live page to a buffer, HASH it, compare to the currently-displayed frame's hash, and push to the panel ONLY if it changed -- structurally prevents needless slow/flashy e-paper refreshes and panel wear. (3) NEW endpoint POST /api/refresh forces an immediate fetch+re-render now, wired to a 'Refresh now' button in the web UI. The hourly poll + change-detection subsumes the midnight date rollover (date flips within the hour); a dedicated midnight tick is optional/deferred. Config gains pollIntervalMinutes (default 60).

CORRECTS the prior refresh-model entry. Change-detection is SEMANTIC, not pixel-based: do NOT render-and-hash to compare. The device tracks the currently-displayed CALENDAR CONTENT (resolved events + date for the live page). On each poll it re-resolves from the feed and compares that content; if it changed -> re-render + push to panel; if unchanged -> do nothing. Publish and POST /api/refresh always re-render + push. (Earlier 'hash the rendered frame' framing is withdrawn.)

Device mDNS hostname is a config value (config.json 'hostname', default 'corkboard' -> http://<hostname>.local/), not hardcoded. config.json now holds: feeds[], pollIntervalMinutes (default 60), hostname (default corkboard).

Text tool + shared bundled fonts. New TextEl (type:'text': text, font, align + shared colour/geometry) and a 'text' tool. Text content edited ON-CANVAS in place (TextOptions panel holds font/align/colour). Fonts: bundled self-hosted open files (NOT Google Fonts CDN) -- device loads them for the Rust renderer AND serves them to the editor as @font-face from one origin, so both shape from the same bytes and it works offline. Shared bundled font MANIFEST (assets/fonts/manifest.json, served at /fonts/manifest.json) lists what's available; NOT config.json. Seed set: Atkinson Hyperlegible (default), Inter, Caveat.

Font manifest format: JSON { fonts: [ { id (stable key stored in TextEl.font), name (picker label), default (bool fallback), faces: [ { weight, style, file } ] } ] }. STATIC font files only, not variable fonts, so the browser and the Rust shaper apply the exact same face. Both consumers read the one file: editor -> @font-face rules (src /fonts/<file>); Rust -> load+register per family/weight/style. v1 may ship Regular-only per family.

Fonts: ship STATIC font files (one file per weight) rather than variable fonts, so neither the browser nor the Rust shaper interpolates the weight axis -> both draw identical outlines (parity). Static/variable is a property of the font FILES, not the manifest. Also recorded: font manifest stays SEPARATE from config.json -- manifest is build-scoped, secret-free, in git, and read by the editor too; config.json is deploy-scoped per-device runtime state with secrets, out of git, device-only. Different lifecycle + trust boundary.

Editor surgery plan 1a complete: migrated the editor document model to the round-two shape — removed the clock element/tool/widget and the timeline (components + store API), dropped clockVariant from the factory + persisted tool-options (with a load() whitelist so stale persisted keys are dropped), and added livePageId (string|null, defaults to first page) with a guarded setLivePage action + null-safe livePage getter. Element types are now calendar|image|drawing. 67/67 tests green, build clean. 5 commits 0136160..4879422. Plans 1b (calendar feed-ref + date variant), 1c (text tool + fonts), 1d (make-live UI) remain. Carry-forward: a future deletePage must reassign livePageId (final-review Minor 1).

Autonomous full-build run (user authorized 'complete everything except hardware deploy'). PROCESS decisions to make the scope tractable in one run: (1) For the cohesive editor phases 1b/1c/1d, dispatch ONE sonnet implementer per phase (the changes are tightly coupled — changing an element's shape cascades through factory/widget/options/tests — so a phase is the natural commit unit) instead of micro-tasks, still strict TDD. (2) Review at PHASE granularity with an opus subagent (not per-micro-task) to fit the full scope; fix Critical/Important before moving on. (3) Reviews use opus subagents, NOT codex (codex parked on first request repeatedly in plan 1a). All commits on main, per phase, stage only own files.

Phase order for the remainder (from the round-two spec build order, minus hardware): 1b editor calendar feed-ref + date variant; 1c editor text tool + bundled fonts + on-canvas editing; 1d editor make-live UI (+ deletePage that reassigns livePageId); then the Rust device server: S1 axum skeleton + Display trait + WebPreview + plain-file storage + full API (document/images/feeds/refresh/preview/static editor); S2 renderer (calendar/date/text/image/drawing -> 800x480 6-colour PNG); S3 calendar ICS fetch+parse + poll + semantic change-detection; S4 parity guardrail (editor screenshot vs preview.png, generous threshold). Panel SPI driver EXCLUDED (hardware).

Bundled fonts seed: shipped ONLY Atkinson Hyperlegible (Regular+Bold, real static TTFs from google/fonts ofl) as public/fonts/atkinson-hyperlegible/{Regular,Bold}.ttf. Inter and Caveat are deferred drop-ins: google/fonts now ships them ONLY as variable fonts, and the design requires STATIC files for editor/Rust shaping parity; obtaining static instances needs fonttools instancing or an unreliable third-party mirror. Spec explicitly permits 'v1 may ship just the Regular face per family', so Atkinson-only is in-bounds. Adding Inter/Caveat later = drop a static TTF in public/fonts/<id>/ + a manifest line. Canonical font location is public/fonts/ (Vite serves at /fonts/); the Rust device will read the same files. Manifest at public/fonts/manifest.json served at /fonts/manifest.json.

Editor surgery COMPLETE (1a-1d). Round-two editor shipped: livePageId + make-live UI + deletePage; calendar feed-reference (feedId + date/today/week variants) with a tolerant deviceApi (fetchFeeds/refreshNow, relative URLs, swallows errors) + feeds store (stub {family}) + Refresh-now button + deterministic sampleCalendar preview; text element + tool with on-canvas (uncontrolled contenteditable, focus-guarded) editing, TextOptions font picker + align, bundled font manifest (public/fonts, Atkinson Hyperlegible seed) loaded via @font-face. Commits 46854ea/38ccc91/288ddd4 + fix 8a039ae. Opus review found 1 Critical (contenteditable caret reset) + 3 Minor, all fixed. 137/137 tests, build clean. Next: Rust device server.

Rust device server layout + crates. Standalone crate at device/ (own Cargo.toml; repo root stays the npm app; gitignore device/target/). Modules: main, config (config.json: feeds[{id,name,secretUrl}], pollIntervalMinutes, hostname), document (serde structs mirroring the editor DocState wire shape; serde ignores editor-only fields), storage (document.json + images/ + GC), display (Display trait + WebPreview holding latest PNG; Panel deferred=hardware), render (page-state->PNG; S1 placeholder, S2 real), api (axum router), state (Arc AppState). Crates: axum 0.8 + tokio + tower-http(ServeDir for the built editor + fallback) + serde/serde_json + image(PNG); S2 adds tiny-skia + ab_glyph for raster/text + 6-colour nearest-palette quantise + stroked-polyline drawings (no-glaring-differences approx of perfect-freehand); S3 adds reqwest(rustls) + an ICS parser (ical/icalendar or minimal hand-roll, decide at S3). Build order S1 skeleton+storage+API+WebPreview+static-serve -> S2 renderer -> S3 calendar feed+poll+semantic change-detection -> S4 parity (Playwright editor screenshot vs preview.png, generous threshold).

Device renderer (S2) working end-to-end. Real raster (tiny-skia + ab_glyph): text (font+align, binary glyph coverage = no AA grey), calendar date/today/week variants matching the editor, image (src id lookup, resize+clamp), drawing (natW/natH scale + x,y offset, AA off), then 6-colour nearest-palette quantise. preview.png serves cached last-render. Opus review found 4 Important + a field-name bug (ImageEl imageId->src) + green-fringe quantise; ALL fixed in 6be877e. 33 cargo tests. Smoke-tested via PUT /api/document + GET /preview.png: blue/black text crisp, centered text, week grid, placed/scaled red drawing — all correct. Commits e2bf8ef + 6be877e.

Device calendar (S3) working end-to-end. Hand-rolled ICS parser (RFC5545 unfolding, 3 DTSTART forms), pure parse_ics/resolve/signature (explicit today, fully tested), reqwest+rustls fetch (secret URL never logged/returned), chrono for real today. render falls back to sample when a calendar's feed is unresolved (keeps parity). Poll loop re-renders ONLY on semantic signature change; POST /api/refresh + PUT /api/document force resolve+render. 56 cargo tests. Smoke-tested: configured a feed at a local ICS server, published a today-calendar referencing it, POST /api/refresh -> preview showed the REAL events (Dentist 10:30, Team sync 14:00) not sample; secret URL absent from logs. Commit ef1f42e.

Editor<->device document sync (1e) + parity guardrail (S4) done. 1e: deviceApi getDocument/putDocument (tolerant), pages.hydrate (defensive), startup hydrate in main.ts, real Publish button (PUT). 148 editor tests. Commit 404cab4. S4: committed Playwright parity test — coarse 40x24 content-mask IoU between the editor surface screenshot and the device preview.png with a fixed feedless doc (both use sample data); IoU 0.416 vs 0.35 floor; npm run test:parity green (6 tests), npm test still 148, no parity tests in jsdom run. Commit 332420c. FULL SYSTEM working end-to-end: editor (137->148 tests) + Rust device (56 tests) + parity (6).

FULL SYSTEM COMPLETE (everything except hardware deploy). Device server built at device\/ (Rust+axum, 63 tests): storage+API+WebPreview, real 6-colour renderer, ICS calendar resolve+poll+semantic change-detection. Editor fully round-two (148 tests) + wired to device (hydrate on load, Publish=PUT). Parity guardrail green (Playwright, IoU 0.416 vs 0.35). Final device review fixed 1 Critical (image path traversal) + 2 Important (ICS UTF-8 panic killing poll loop; today missing from change-signature). Smoke-tested end-to-end incl. real ICS feed resolution into preview.png. Only deferred: Panel SPI driver (hardware), editor image-upload UI wiring, Inter\/Caveat fonts, a few low-risk Minors.

Editor UX round: (1) removed TopBar 'Refresh now' — Publish always PUTs the doc and device PUT /api/document re-resolves feeds + re-renders, so a no-op Publish is the refresh; (2) text editing is now double-click-to-edit (EditorCanvas owns editingElId; MovableElement skips drag-preventDefault while editing so the caret lands; TextWidget contenteditable+auto-focus+stop-editing on blur; new text boxes auto-edit); (3) Backspace/Delete guard uses closest('[contenteditable=true]') so editing removes chars not the element (jsdom isContentEditable unreliable); (4) DrawOptions pen sizes shown as sized circles. 154 Vitest green, build clean, verified in-browser.

Editor UI round: removed per-page Make-live (Publish now promotes the selected page to live) + added a Preview link to /preview.png; wired image upload end-to-end (ImageOptions → POST /api/images, src=id, display via /api/images/{id}); added z-order (bringToFront/sendToBack) buttons; added a background tool with per-page Page.background rendered by both editor and device.

Calendar fix: ICS parser ignored RRULE so recurring Google-calendar events never showed (only one-off events appeared). Added RRULE/EXDATE parsing + recurrence expansion in resolve() (FREQ DAILY/WEEKLY/MONTHLY/YEARLY, INTERVAL/COUNT/UNTIL/BYDAY/EXDATE; WKST ignored). Also reordered the poll task to resolve-then-sleep so real data shows on startup instead of after the first interval. Verified end-to-end against the real family feed: weekly Recorder (Thu) and Rosie choir (Tue) now render.
Renderer: Floyd-Steinberg dither images to the 6-colour palette; Lanczos3 resize + alpha-composite for PNG transparency; text/strokes stay exact-palette so only images dither.

Text auto-fits its box (largest size that wraps within w/h): device text::fit_font_size, editor src/lib/textFit.ts via real-DOM measurement. Replaces min(w,h)*0.25. Editor recomputes after webfont load (fallback metrics oversize by ~1px).
Image tool is now one-click: file dialog -> upload -> centred aspect-correct element (imagePlacement), select reactivated. Removed ImageOptions + toolOptions.imageId. Resize locks to image aspect.

mex tidy: fixed ./data path + ImageOptions.vue stale ref, fixed patterns/INDEX broken example links, refreshed stack.md (device built). Drift 42->82. Remaining 6 DEPENDENCY_MISSING warnings are false positives (axum is Cargo-only; others are prose, not deps).
Orientation is now a per-page property (Page.orientation), not document-level; legacy doc-level orientation kept as a load-time migration fallback (editor hydrate + device serde).

Calendar 'week' grid redesigned into a shared 7-day 'agenda' list (Today/Tomorrow/weekday headings, indented 12h events, no boxes/dividers); variant renamed week->agenda with serde alias + load migration; render.rs draw_agenda and CalendarWidget.vue share one sizing formula for WYSIWYG.

Device text now rendered with FreeType monochrome (FT_LOAD_TARGET_MONO) via freetype-rs[bundled], replacing ab_glyph — ab_glyph had no hinting so 1-bit text was jagged. Fonts stores bytes; faces built per-render (text::Faces). Parity IoU 0.46→0.65.

Added 3 selectable fonts (open, OFL/permissive): DejaVu Sans (open Verdana), Carlito (open Calibri), Gelasio (metric-compatible Georgia). Verified crisp at small 1-bit via FreeType mono. Manifest-driven: files in public/fonts/, entries in manifest.json + DEFAULT_MANIFEST fallback. Atkinson stays default.
Removed the live-preview long-poll page and the dashboard's 5s /api/status poll to cut device idle power draw; editor's Preview button now opens /preview.png directly
